title: SBC Pastors Publish "Resolution on Abolishing Abortion" for 2021 Convention Meeting
author: Sam Riley
orientation: A coalition of Southern Baptist pastors announced a Resolution on Abolishing Abortion to be introduced at the 2021 SBC annual meeting, calling on the convention to repent of compromise, reject incrementalism, and demand the immediate abolition of abortion without exception. The article frames the move as a major shift away from the secular, wait-on-the-Supreme-Court strategy of the mainstream Pro-Life Movement.
audiences: Pro-life advocate weighing incrementalism vs. immediatism
Pro-choice or secular reader curious about the theology

questions.question: What did the coalition of Southern Baptist pastors actually announce, and when will the resolution be considered?
questions.answer: On Tuesday, June 9, 2020, a national coalition of SBC pastors unveiled a Resolution on Abolishing Abortion, along with a dedicated website and Facebook page, with the goal of having it introduced at the 2021 SBC annual meeting in Nashville.
questions.quote: On Tuesday, a coalition of Southern Baptist pastors from across the country announced a [resolution](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScgVHlX3klYqQthBEJ-6YGgANtFuNaLjYXiQ6loUzaHOLu8OA/viewform) to abolish abortion in America as well the launch of an accompanying [website](https://southernbaptistsabolishingabortion.com/?fbclid=IwAR0hEkrjkSxhaUdJ-7TSkF6kWr0AZNB2RZX0jkAJXA845mGsib5KiGxVteU) and [Facebook page](https://www.facebook.com/SBAbolitionists/). The resolution will be introduced at the 2021 Southern Baptist Convention’s annual meeting.

questions.question: Why does the article say this is a significant moment for the broader church, not just the SBC?
questions.answer: Because the SBC carries enormous influence among Christians worldwide, an SBC-level commitment to abolition would echo far beyond Southern Baptist pews. The author also frames it as part of a wave that started with Oklahoma Baptists' 2019 resolution calling for abolition without exception or compromise.
questions.quote: Due to the considerable sway the Southern Baptist Convention (SBC) has with Christians nation and worldwide, this is a big development. It comes on the heels of Oklahoma Baptists (the SBC-affiliated convention in Oklahoma) passing a [resolution](https://freethestates.org/2019/11/oklahoma-baptists-resolution/index.html) calling for abortion’s abolition “without exception or compromise” in Oklahoma last November.

questions.question: What previous abolitionist effort inside the SBC does the article point to, and what happened to it?
questions.answer: Indiana Pastor Derin Stidd, who is featured in the new resolution's announcement video, made an abolitionist motion at the 2019 National SBC annual meeting that was killed on procedural grounds rather than on its merits.
questions.quote: Notably, Indiana Pastor Derin Stidd – who is featured in the video announcement of the resolution – made an abolitionist motion at the 2019 National Southern Baptist Convention annual meeting. The motion was [struck down](https://freethestates.org/2019/08/squashed-like-a-bug/index.html) on a technicality.

questions.question: How does the article distinguish abolitionism from the mainstream Pro-Life Movement?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate weighing incrementalism vs. immediatism
questions.answer: Abolitionism treats abortion as sin demanding a Gospel response, while pro-life leaders are described as relying on secular arguments and political compromise. The article argues this difference is not stylistic but theological: sin requires repentance, not regulation.
questions.quote: Abortion is sin, and the answer to sin is the Gospel. This stands in stark contrast to the leading politicians and organizations in the Pro-Life Movement who have fought abortion with worldly arguments and by worldly political strategies which consider compromise as the key to success.

questions.question: What is the abolitionist critique of the "just wait on the Supreme Court" strategy?
questions.answer: The article casts the dominant pro-life political playbook as a perpetual delay tactic that surrenders to Roe and refuses to demand immediate justice. The Resolution on Abolishing Abortion is presented as an explicit call for the SBC to stop waiting and instead exercise prophetic, immediate action.
questions.quote: The political strategy practiced by the leading pro-life politicians and lobbyists can be summed up in two words: “Just wait.” We are to wait until the Supreme Court changes their opinion, according to the pro-life leaders. The Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion is a call for the SBC to stop waiting.

questions.question: What does the resolution say governing authorities must do when higher courts legalize abortion?
questions.answer: It asserts that lower governing authorities have a duty before God to interpose, ignore, or nullify iniquitous decisions like Roe v. Wade rather than defer to them. This explicitly invites state and local officials to defy federal court rulings that sanction the killing of preborn children.
questions.quote: *(10) WHEREAS, governing authorities at every level have a duty before God to uphold justice asserting their God-ordained and constitutional authority to establish equal protection under the law for all, born and preborn, by intervening, ignoring, or nullifying iniquitous decisions when other authorities condone such injustices as the legal taking of innocent life (Daniel 3; 1 Kings 12; 2 Kings 11; Jeremiah 26:10-16; 36:9-31; 37:11- 21; 39:7-10), and*

questions.question: Why does the resolution explicitly reject incremental pro-life laws?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate weighing incrementalism vs. immediatism
questions.answer: Because, in the resolution's view, decades of incremental laws have failed to establish equal protection and have instead codified the conditions under which abortion is legally permitted. Incrementalism is treated as a theological problem, not just a tactical one, because it asks God to settle for partial repentance.
questions.quote: *(11) WHEREAS, over the past 48 years with 60+ million abortions, traditional Pro-life laws, though well intended, have not established equal protection and justice for the preborn, but on the contrary, appallingly have established incremental, regulatory guidelines for when, where, why, and how to obtain legal abortion of innocent preborn children, thereby legally sanctioning abortion, and*

questions.question: How does the resolution describe the SBC's own track record on abortion?
questions.answer: It admits the SBC has passed many resolutions affirming the value of human life since 1980 but has never taken the final step of demanding immediate abolition without exception or compromise. The new resolution frames this as incomplete repentance that needs to be corrected.
questions.quote: *(12) WHEREAS, since 1980, the SBC has passed many resolutions reaffirming the importance of human life at all stages of development, our repentance is still lacking as we consistently have stopped short of calling for the immediate abolition of abortion without exception or compromise, and*

questions.question: What does the resolution declare about exceptions to legal protection for the preborn?
questions.audience: Pro-choice or secular reader curious about the theology
questions.answer: It states unequivocally that abortion is murder and rejects every category of exception, including those grounded in circumstance, mental or physical development, or method of conception. There is no carve-out for rape, incest, or fetal anomaly under this framework.
questions.quote: *(14) RESOLVED, that the messengers of the SBC meeting in Nashville, Tennessee, June 15-16, 2021, do state unequivocally that abortion is murder, and we reject any position that allows for any exceptions to the legal protection of our preborn neighbors, compromises God’s holy standard of justice, or promotes any God-hating partiality (Psa 94:6; Isa 10:1-2; Prov 24:11; Psa 82:1-4), and be it further*

questions.question: What does the resolution say should happen to those who commit or perform abortion?
questions.answer: It calls for the murder of preborn children to be punished equally under the law, treating it as a crime against humanity rather than a regulated medical procedure. This is one of the most concrete policy demands embedded in the resolution.
questions.quote: *(16) RESOLVED, that we affirm that the murder of preborn children is a crime against humanity that must be punished equally under the law, and be it further*

questions.question: How does the resolution want pastors and SBC leaders to use their pulpits?
questions.answer: It calls on pastors and entity leaders to deploy their gifts of preaching, teaching, and leading with one unified, prophetic voice to abolish abortion. The expectation is convention-wide engagement, not silence or polite policy statements.
questions.quote: *(18) RESOLVED, that as Southern Baptists we will engage, with God’s help, in establishing equal justice and protection for the preborn according to the authority of God’s Word as well as local and federal law, and call upon pastors and entity leaders to use their God-given gifts of preaching, teaching, and leading with one unified, principled, prophetic voice to abolish abortion, and be it finally*

questions.question: What theological framing does the announcement video give to abortion?
questions.audience: Pro-choice or secular reader curious about the theology
questions.answer: The video calls abortion a modern-day holocaust and an assault on God's glory, citing 60 million abortions since Roe v. Wade and casting it as an attempt to usurp God's sovereignty. It explicitly invokes the language of God hating the shedding of innocent blood.
questions.quote: Since the Roe vs Wade Supreme Court decision in 1973, over 60 million babies have been led to slaughter in our nation alone. Abortion destroys the life of the most innocent, vulnerable among us, whom God has knitted together in their mother’s womb from fertilization. Abortion is an assault on God’s glory and an aim to usurp God’s sovereignty. God hates abortion.

questions.question: What scale does the video claim for the SBC's potential impact?
questions.answer: It cites a membership of 15 million people across over 47,000 churches and urges them to act with one collective voice for abolition without exception or compromise. The framing is that an SBC-aligned movement represents one of the largest mobilizable Christian blocs in the country.
questions.quote: Southern Baptists have a membership of 15 million men and women and represent over 47,000 churches. It’s time we rise up with one collective voice, pray for God’s mercy and forgiveness, pray that He would heal our land, and act now to abolish abortion in our land, without exception or compromise.

questions.question: What does the article identify as the historical call of the Abolitionist Movement since 2010?
questions.answer: The movement's central call has been for Christians to repent of apathy toward preborn image bearers and of complicity with regulatory abortion laws. Repentance and refusing to compromise with abortion are framed as the spiritual heart of the resolution.
questions.quote: The call of the Abolitionist Movement since its reignition in 2010 has been for Christians individually and the Body of Christ collectively to repent of our apathy toward the thousands of preborn image bearers who are murdered daily in the U.S. alone, and of our comfort with supporting laws regulating abortion.

related.title: Former ERLC Policy Director Matt Hawkins Glad SBC Squashed Abolition "Like a Bug"
related.reason: Background on the 2019 abolitionist motion by Derin Stidd that was killed on a technicality, which this article cites as the precursor to the new resolution effort.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: The 2019 Oklahoma Baptists resolution explicitly cited in this article as the catalyst that the new national SBC effort builds on.

related.title: Prominent Southern Baptist Voice Calls For Total Abolition
related.reason: Earlier 2020 piece tracking the same intra-SBC shift toward abolitionism, featuring overlapping figures like Stidd and the Ascol brothers.

related.title: Southern Baptist Convention Moves One Step Closer to Abolitionism (And Answering Objections)
related.reason: Direct follow-up reporting on what happened to this very resolution at the 2021 SBC annual meeting.

related.title: Abolitionists Pass Resolution*, Make Huge Strides at SBC Convention
related.reason: Companion 2021 report on the modified resolution that ultimately passed at the SBC, closing the loop on this announcement.

related.title: The Rise and Progress of Abortion Abolitionism in the Southern Baptist Convention
related.reason: Longer historical narrative placing this 2020 announcement inside the multi-year arc of SBC abolitionism.

related.title: Arkansas Baptists Pass Resolution on Abolishing Abortion at State Convention
related.reason: Concrete example of the state-by-state spread of the same Resolution on Abolishing Abortion that this article calls for at the national level.
